steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage opens by noting researchers once assumed cuttlefish lacked 3D vision, then describes Mercer's team testing that assumption with 3D glasses. The passage closes with the finding: cuttlefish changed their striking position in response to 3D images, “suggesting that their vision is more like that of sheep and macaque monkeys than that of octopuses or squid.” The main idea is the reversal from assumption to discovery.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Cuttlefish eyes are much more complex than those of either sheep or macaque monkeys.": The passage compares cuttlefish vision favorably to sheep and macaques — it never claims cuttlefish eyes are more complex. ✗
  • "Before completing their study, Mercer's team showed that sheep have 3D vision, but macaque monkeys may not.": The passage does not say Mercer's team studied sheep or macaques at all, and it treats both as having established 3D vision. ✗
  • "Mercer's team designed 3D glasses for cuttlefish.": The 3D glasses were a method, not the finding. Focusing only on the method misses the main idea. ✗
  • "The findings of Mercer's team's study suggest that like sheep and macaque monkeys, cuttlefish may also have 3D vision.": This matches the passage exactly — the team's evidence suggests cuttlefish have 3D vision comparable to sheep and macaques. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
